learnings.question: What percentage of newly learned information did psychologist Hermann Ebbinghaus prove is forgotten within 24 hours without active recall?
learnings.learning: In the 1880s, Hermann Ebbinghaus proved that humans forget about 80% of what they learn within 24 hours unless they actively force themselves to recall it.
learnings.articleTitle: Is Your Brain the Worst Place to Store Ideas? Why I Built a Second One

learnings.question: When analyzing the vocabulary of the Quran, approximately what cumulative percentage of the total text is made up of the top 20 most frequently repeated words?
learnings.learning: Applying the Pareto Principle to the Quran reveals that the top 20 most frequent words do not make up 80% of the text, but instead accumulate to approximately 20.83%.
learnings.articleTitle: Top 20 Words to Understand the Quran: A Data Analysis using Python

learnings.question: Which concept refers to the actual occurrence and unfolding of events in a person's life that can be influenced or altered by their decisions and supplications?
learnings.learning: In Islamic theology, Qada’ represents the actual unfolding of events in a person's life which can be altered through actions and supplication, whereas Qadr is the complete, preordained plan eternally known to Allah.
learnings.articleTitle: The Art of Qada’ and Qadr

learnings.question: Which Scrum artifact is used to visualize the progress of a Sprint by displaying the remaining work to help track if the team is on schedule?
learnings.learning: A Burndown Chart is a visual Scrum artifact that displays the remaining work in a Sprint to track whether the development team is on schedule.

learnings.question: In Islamic belief, what three primary roles define a person's purposeful journey and responsibility on Earth?
options: Isolating from society, performing only physical rituals, and avoiding community leadership
Eliminating personal accountability, rejecting free will, and pursuing material perfection
Seeking worldly wealth, building physical monuments, and achieving political dominance
Worshiping Allah, serving as a caretaker (khalifah) of the Earth, and living life as a test of faith and conduct
learnings.learning: In Islamic belief, life is a purposeful journey defined by three core roles: worshiping Allah, serving as a caretaker (khalifah) of the Earth, and treating life as a test of faith and conduct to prepare for the hereafter.
